text:
In a forgotten valley nestled between two mountains, there lived a wise old owl who knew everything. Every dawn, he'd watch old leaves turn. One day, a new leaf fell. The old owl hooted softly.

"Maybe it's time?"

No one listened.

That night, a storm roared. Rain fell harder than ever. Underneath an oak leaf, tiny feet walked. A mouse hopped.

"A friend!"

The mouse screeched back. Together they found an old arrowhead.

"Hawks soar high. I see them."

"I want to fly like them!"

They flew south. Months passed. They found golden honey.

"You're brave."

"I'm curious."

They soared over fields. The sun went down. Stars twinkled.

"You're my star."

They didn’d want to go home.

"We're stars, forever."

They were. Far away o-stars twinkled. That's where they'd stay. And they were glad.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
